meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Debugging Setup for Students (Intermediate Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Master Claude Code and Antigravity to streamline your development process. An intermediate checklist for student AI builders.
content.answer_block: To overcome debugging bottlenecks, integrate Claude Code for autonomous reasoning and Antigravity for structured code analysis. By combining these, you transform error logs into actionable fix-plans, allowing you to bypass manual syntax hunting and focus on architectural integrity. This workflow effectively turns your terminal into a senior-level pair programming session.
content.intro:
The screen flickers. You’ve been staring at a recursive error for three hours, the stack trace is a labyrinth, and your motivation is rapidly evaporating. Every student developer knows this specific brand of exhaustion—the feeling that you are hitting a wall that only senior engineers can climb. 

But the wall is an illusion. The modern AI builder doesn't fix bugs manually; they orchestrate agents to dismantle them. By pairing Claude Code’s reasoning engine with Antigravity’s analytical precision, you bridge the gap between amateur code and production-grade software.

steps.title: Initialize the Agentic Environment
steps.description: Establish a baseline where Claude Code understands your project structure before attempting complex repairs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project structure and identify the entry point and core dependencies for this repository.

steps.title: Contextual Error Injection
steps.description: Feed specific error logs into the system to allow the AI to map the failure to the codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: I have a recurring error in my stack trace. Map this error to the likely offending modules in my source code.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file [filename] --prompt 'Explain the root cause of the error in this file'

steps.title: Automated Hypothesis Testing
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate potential fix strategies without applying them to the main branch yet.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose three distinct architectural solutions to resolve the identified bug, weighing trade-offs for performance.

steps.title: Safe Implementation & Verification
steps.description: Execute the fix and verify it against the existing test suite to ensure no regressions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the proposed fix against our coding standards and verify if it covers all edge cases.

steps.title: Post-Mortem Documentation
steps.description: Update your technical logs so you never face this specific bug again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a concise technical summary of the bug, the root cause, and the fix for my project README.

pro_tips: Always run your test suite immediately after a Claude-suggested fix to catch hidden side effects.
Use the 'claude config' command to set custom persona instructions that mimic a senior staff engineer.
Keep your context window clean by deleting temporary debug files before running deep analysis prompts.
When facing complex logic errors, ask Claude to visualize the state change before writing any code.
Use Antigravity to identify 'code smells' during the debugging phase to prevent future technical debt.
content.next_steps: After mastering these debugging workflows, begin integrating CI/CD pipelines to trigger these Claude Code scripts automatically upon test failure.
content.conversion_block: Your journey from student to professional AI builder starts with how you handle failure. By mastering these tools, you aren't just fixing bugs—you're learning how to architect systems that thrive. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
